indication: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, coronary artery disease, assess for ischemia and interval change in LV function
technique: Pharmacologic stress echocardiography was performed using dobutamine infusion protocol (5-10-20-30-40 mcg/kg/min) with atropine augmentation. Resting and peak stress 2D and Doppler echocardiographic images were obtained.

protocol.symptoms: Mild palpitations at peak dose, no chest pain or dyspnea
protocol.ecg: Baseline: Atrial fibrillation with controlled ventricular response. Stress: Atrial fibrillation with rate to 118 bpm. No ischemic ST changes.

restingImages.leftVentricle: Moderately dilated (LVIDd 6.0 cm). Global hypokinesis. Akinesis of the inferior wall. Hypokinesis of the inferolateral and lateral walls.
restingImages.valves: Moderate mitral regurgitation (3+). Aortic valve sclerosis without stenosis. Mild tricuspid regurgitation.
restingImages.rightVentricle: Normal size, mildly reduced function (TAPSE 1.7 cm).

stressImages.wallMotion: Inferior wall remains akinetic. Inferolateral wall remains hypokinetic without significant change. No new wall motion abnormalities. Anterior and anteroseptal walls demonstrate appropriate augmentation.
stressImages.ejectionFraction: 33-38% at peak stress (mild improvement from rest)
stressImages.contractileReserve: Mild contractile reserve present in the anterior, anteroseptal, and lateral segments.

doppler.mitralRegurgitation: Moderate at rest, unchanged with stress.

impression: Moderately dilated left ventricle with severely reduced systolic function at rest (EF 30-35%).
Fixed wall motion abnormality of the inferior wall (akinesis), consistent with prior infarction/scar.
No inducible ischemia. No new wall motion abnormalities at peak stress.
Mild contractile reserve in the non-infarcted segments, suggesting some viability.
Moderate mitral regurgitation, stable with stress.
Severely dilated left atrium. Estimated RVSP 40 mmHg.
Compared to prior stress echocardiogram (2022-01-20), no significant change in LV function or wall motion.
recommendations: Continue guideline-directed medical therapy for HFrEF
No evidence of significant inducible ischemia to warrant revascularization at this time
comparison: Stress echocardiogram dated 2022-01-20 and TTE dated 2020-09-21. LV function and wall motion abnormalities are stable.
